options.a: horizontal component of the Earth’s magnetic field is too small to permit the use of a magnetic compass.
options.b: angle of dip is minimal whereby a freely suspended compass needle will be almost fully vertically aligned.
options.c: angle of dip is maximal whereby a freely suspended compass needle will be almost fully horizontally aligned.
options.d: vertical component of the Earth’s magnetic field is too small to permit the use of a magnetic compass.
